channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Islamabad Traffic Police implements special plan for Eid ul Adha cattle markets
item.description: Islamabad Traffic Police (ITP) has finalised a detailed traffic management plan for the upcoming Eid ul Adha, specifically targeting smooth movement around the city's cattle markets. Chief Traffic Off...

item.title: University of Dar es Salaam introduces digital platform to guide postgraduate researchers
item.description: The University of Dar es Salaam has unveiled a new digital platform called SKN Research Compass, designed to help postgraduate students overcome common research challenges. Many Tanzanian students fac...

item.title: Kenya court exempts adolescents from certain sexual offence charges for consensual relationships
item.description: On Wednesday, a Kenyan court ruled that some sections of the Sexual Offences Act cannot be applied to minors who engage in consensual sex. The judgement came after rights groups, including the Centre ...

item.title: Lawyers challenge DOJ directive on arresting Senator Bato dela Rosa
item.description: The legal team of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa has formally questioned the Department of Justice's (DOJ) directive to arrest him, citing concerns over due process and constitutional compliance. Dur...

item.title: IGP Approves Redeployment of Police Mobile Force Officers on Health and Compassionate Grounds
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, Tunji Disu, don give approval make some inspectors and other officers for Nigeria Police Mobile Force (PMF) demobilise from their squadrons and return to their respect...

item.title: Documents show Queen Elizabeth supported Andrew’s appointment as UK trade envoy despite preferences for private leisure
item.description: Newly released documents reveal that Queen Elizabeth was strongly in favour of her son, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, taking on a prominent role as the UK’s special representative for international trad...

item.title: Chiefs’ McCarthy credits boxing, Moldavia stint and mental coaching for personal and professional growth
item.description: Aden McCarthy, the 22-year-old rising star of Kaizer Chiefs, credits his development both on and off the field to a combination of unique experiences. A graduate of the Chiefs youth academy, McCarthy ...

item.title: High Court Confirms Dari Business Park Sale, Orders Sh50 Million Deposit to Protect Entim Sidai
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has upheld the sale of former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju's Dari Business Park, ruling that the property’s auction and transfer to a third party cannot be reversed. Justic...

item.title: Israel deports foreign activists from Gaza-bound flotilla after detention
item.description: On Thursday, Israel deported all foreign activists who were detained from a flotilla attempting to reach Gaza, following international criticism over their treatment. Israel’s foreign ministry spokesm...

item.title: Man stands trial over fatal amputation in Innisfail park
item.description: A Cairns Supreme Court jury is hearing the trial of John Yalu, 41, charged with the murder of 66-year-old Kalman Tal in Innisfail, Far North Queensland. Yalu has pleaded not guilty to murder but admit...

item.title: North American markets dip as Treasury yields climb amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: North American stock markets opened lower on Thursday, driven by rising oil prices and climbing U.S. Treasury yields amid continued tensions between Iran and the United States. Higher bond yields can ...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Africa's First Xi Jinping Thought Research Centre
item.description: Tanzania and China have strengthened their political partnership with the opening of the Research Centre for Xi Jinping Thought on Socialism with Chinese Characteristics for a New Era at the Mwalimu J...

item.title: Dunkin’ to release 48oz cold beverage buckets in select US stores for Memorial Day Weekend
item.description: Dunkin’ Donuts is set to offer its popular 48-ounce cold beverage buckets in participating stores across the United States starting this Friday. Initially tested in February in Massachusetts and New H...

item.title: A woman explores love, identity, and freedom through the circus
item.description: This short fiction piece narrates the inner life of Sita, a woman trapped in a conventional marriage with Heron, a man who is attentive yet emotionally distant. From her childhood fascination with the...

item.title: McCarthy credits boxing, Moldavian trials and mental coaching for shaping his career
item.description: Aden McCarthy, one of Kaizer Chiefs’ standout players this season, has opened up about the experiences off the pitch that helped shape him as both a player and a person. The 22-year-old, who came thro...

item.title: Punjab Government Enforces Section 144 Around Airports and Air Force Bases for Aviation Safety
item.description: The Punjab government has imposed Section 144 across all airports and Pakistan Air Force (PAF) bases in the province for a period of 30 days, according to a notification issued by the Home Department ...

item.title: Governor Radda Confirmed As APC Consensus Candidate For Katsina 2027 Election
item.description: Governor Dikko Radda don officially secure di All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship ticket for Katsina State ahead of 2027 election. On Thursday, all 2,835 party delegates for Katsina unanimous...

item.title: The History and Legacy of the Hardy Tree at St Pancras Old Church
item.description: The Hardy Tree was a famous ash tree in the churchyard of St Pancras Old Church, London, known for the dense cluster of Victorian gravestones encircling its base. Traditionally, the arrangement of the...

item.title: Kenya to Play in 2027 Afcon Qualifiers Despite Automatic Host Spot
item.description: After the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) qualifiers draw on May 19, 2026 in Cairo, many Kenyans were puzzled why Harambee Stars will still feature in the qualifiers despite already having a guaran...

item.title: Over 15 Drivers Confirmed for Mkwawa Water Rally in Iringa
item.description: More than 15 drivers are set to compete in the Mkwawa Pure Drinking Water Rally next month in Iringa Region, Tanzania. Organisers, led by Robert Maneno, chairman of Iringa Region Motorsport, said prep...
